channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: NASA administrator hails 'Golden Age' of lunar exploration as Moon Base plans unveiled - Live Science
item.description:
1. NASA administrator hails 'Golden Age' of lunar exploration as Moon Base plans unveiled  Live Science
  2. NASA lays out moon base plans with landers, buggies and drones at the top of the list  NPR
  3. NASA picks Blue Origin to deliver lunar rovers to the moon  The Washington Post
  4. NASA outlines nearly $1 billion investment into initial Moon Base missions  Spaceflight Now
  5. Artemis moon base will cover 'hundreds of square miles' with hopping drones and new lunar rovers, NASA says  Space

item.title: A severed piece of sea cucumber refused to die, and what happened next could transform medicine - Phys.org
item.description:
1. A severed piece of sea cucumber refused to die, and what happened next could transform medicine  Phys.org

  3. Can Cells from a Sea Cucumber Live Forever?  Yahoo
  4. Sea Cucumber Tissues Demonstrate Natural Immortality in Seawater  Genetic Engineering and Biotechnology News

item.description:

  4. Astronomers weighed a 'little red dot' discovered by the James Webb telescope — and found a 'naked' black hole inside  Live Science
  5. Webb reveals black hole that formed before its galaxy  European Space Agency

item.title: It’s a once in a Blue Moon moment on May 31 - WGAL
item.description:
1. It’s a once in a Blue Moon moment on May 31  WGAL
  2. Look up: Rare Blue Moon and 4 planets will shine across the sky at the end of May  FOX Weather

  4. What is the blue-white star next to the moon tonight?  Space
  5. A rare blue ‘micromoon’ is rising at the end of May  National Geographic

item.title: Paleontologists in New Mexico have just dug up a two-legged, beak-mouthed reptile that looks almost exactly like a small dinosaur — except it lived more than a hundred million years before any dinosaur evolved that body plan, and it wasn't a dinosaur at all, - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Paleontologists in New Mexico have just dug up a two-legged, beak-mouthed reptile that looks almost exactly like a small dinosaur — except it lived more than a hundred million years before any dinosaur evolved that body plan, and it wasn't a dinosaur at all,  Space Daily
  2. New species of bizarre, bipedal, toothless crocodile relative from the Triassic discovered  EurekAlert!
  3. These Ancient Crocodiles Walked On Two Legs And Had Beaks!  DOGO News
  4. This Toothless, Beaked Crocodile Ancestor Walked on Two Legs  Nautilus | Science
  5. Bizarre ‘witch croc’ that looks just like a dinosaur discovered in New Mexico  BBC Wildlife Magazine

item.title: Experimental randomness amplification - Nature
item.description:

  2. Perfect randomness realized for the first time  Phys.org
  3. A quantum computing system’s perfect randomness could keep your secrets safe  Scientific American
  4. ETH Zurich’s Qubits Yield First Certifiably Perfect Randomness  Quantum Zeitgeist
  5. Scientists create perfectly random numbers using entangled quantum chips for first time  Interesting Engineering

  4. Black Hole Discoveries Abound in Newly Released Catalog  Columbia University

item.title: Scientists hung onto woolly mammoth fossils for 70 years — then discovered a huge mistake: 'Something was amiss' - New York Post
item.description:
1. Scientists hung onto woolly mammoth fossils for 70 years — then discovered a huge mistake: 'Something was amiss'  New York Post
  2. 'Mammoth' Bones Kept in a Museum For 70 Years Turn Out to Be Entirely Different Animal  ScienceAlert
  3. For 70 years, scientists thought these were mammoth bones until they discovered they belonged to an entirely different animal  MSN

item.title: The asteroid that ended the dinosaurs happened to strike a shallow seabed rich in sulphur and buried hydrocarbons, throwing soot and aerosols into the sky — and researchers argue the same rock hitting most other places might not have ended their reign. - Space Daily
item.description: The asteroid that ended the dinosaurs happened to strike a shallow seabed rich in sulphur and buried hydrocarbons, throwing soot and aerosols into the sky — and researchers argue the same rock hitting most other places might not have ended their reign.  Space Daily

item.description:

item.title: When Soviet engineers landed Venera 13 on the surface of Venus in 1982, they expected the probe to survive maybe thirty minutes in the planet's lead-melting heat and crushing pressure — it survived 127 minutes and sent back the first color photographs eve - Space Daily

item.title: Two radio astronomers spent months trying to eliminate a faint hiss in their antenna, even scrubbing out pigeon droppings, before realising the noise they couldn’t get rid of was the afterglow of the early universe — the cosmic microwave background left behin - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Two radio astronomers spent months trying to eliminate a faint hiss in their antenna, even scrubbing out pigeon droppings, before realising the noise they couldn’t get rid of was the afterglow of the early universe — the cosmic microwave background left behin  Space Daily
  2. In 1964, two astronomers cleaned pigeon mess from an antenna and found the echo of the Big Bang  The Times of India
  3. A slice of the static on an old untuned television was the afterglow of the Big Bang, which means millions of people spent decades staring at the oldest light in the universe without knowing it.  Space Daily

item.title: The Milky Way may have devoured another galaxy named Loki, and astronomers think they've found its remains - Space
item.description:
1. The Milky Way may have devoured another galaxy named Loki, and astronomers think they've found its remains  Space
  2. The Milky Way ate another galaxy. Scientists say they’ve found the scraps  CNN
  3. Scientists think they may have found the remains of an entire galaxy that the Milky Way absorbed billions of years ago — 20 unusual stars hiding inside our own galactic disk, possibly the leftovers of a small galaxy our own home swallowed when it was still you  Space Daily
  4. Astronomers trace 20 Milky Way stars to ancient dwarf galaxy named ‘Loki’  Mid-day
  5. NASA Discovers Eerie Remains Of A Galaxy The Milky Way Devoured  HotHardware

item.title: The Opportunity rover's famous last words — "my battery is low and it's getting dark" — were never actually sent from Mars. The real story of where that sentence came from is stranger, and somehow sadder - Space Daily
item.description:

  2. NASA’s Opportunity rover sent haunting final message after 15 years on Mars  MSN
  3. The Opportunity rover survived on Mars for 14 years — roughly 55 times longer than its 90-day design mission — before a 2018 global dust storm so vast it turned "day into night" across the entire planet buried its solar panels, and despite NASA sending more t  Space Daily

item.title: In 1919, British astronomer Arthur Eddington sailed to the island of Príncipe to photograph a total solar eclipse, measuring starlight bent by the Sun’s gravity — a result that confirmed a key prediction of Einstein’s general relativity and helped turn him into the m - Space Daily
item.description:

item.title: When NASA's 77-tonne Skylab station fell out of orbit in 1979 and scattered debris across Western Australia, the Shire of Esperance did the only reasonable thing: it fined the United States $400 for littering. - Space Daily

item.title: In 1998 ground controllers lost contact with the Sun-watching SOHO spacecraft and feared it might be gone for good, until the Arecibo radio telescope in Puerto Rico pinged the silent, slowly tumbling craft — beginning a months-long effort to coax it back to life. - Space Daily
item.description: In 1998 ground controllers lost contact with the Sun-watching SOHO spacecraft and feared it might be gone for good, until the Arecibo radio telescope in Puerto Rico pinged the silent, slowly tumbling craft — beginning a months-long effort to coax it back to life.  Space Daily

item.title: When the Galileo spacecraft's main antenna failed to unfurl on the way to Jupiter, engineers salvaged the mission by rewriting its software across deep space, compressing its data and squeezing a flagship science return through a low-gain antenna never me - Space Daily
item.description: When the Galileo spacecraft's main antenna failed to unfurl on the way to Jupiter, engineers salvaged the mission by rewriting its software across deep space, compressing its data and squeezing a flagship science return through a low-gain antenna never me  Space Daily

item.title: Triply-eclipsing triple star system discovered with TESS - Phys.org
item.description: Triply-eclipsing triple star system discovered with TESS  Phys.org

item.title: Voyager 2 flew past Neptune in 1989 and detected faint hints of auroras it couldn't explain — because the magnetic field is tilted 47 degrees off the rotation axis and the auroras were glowing in entirely the wrong place - Space Daily
item.description: Voyager 2 flew past Neptune in 1989 and detected faint hints of auroras it couldn't explain — because the magnetic field is tilted 47 degrees off the rotation axis and the auroras were glowing in entirely the wrong place  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists May Have Been Wrong About Europa’s Water Plumes - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Scientists May Have Been Wrong About Europa’s Water Plumes  Yahoo
  2. Scientists thought Jupiter's moon Europa was ejecting water. Now they're not so sure  Space
  3. It Looks Like Europa Doesn't Have Plumes of Water Vapour After All  Universe Today
  4. Investigating Europa (IMAGE)  EurekAlert!
  5. The case for water plumes on Europa just got much harder to make  AOL.com

item.title: Ayisha Ashruf and her colleagues at Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre tracked seventeen pieces of 1960s-era space junk for 36 years and found that once the Sun's sunspot count climbs past roughly two-thirds of a cycle's peak, the upper atmosphere starts pulli - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Ayisha Ashruf and her colleagues at Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre tracked seventeen pieces of 1960s-era space junk for 36 years and found that once the Sun's sunspot count climbs past roughly two-thirds of a cycle's peak, the upper atmosphere starts pulli  Space Daily
  2. Study Shows How Sunspot Activity Speeds Up Reentries  Universe Today
  3. A 36-year sweep of orbital debris has found the solar threshold where Earth’s upper atmosphere starts pulling space junk down faster, just as mega-constellations crowd low orbit  Space Daily

item.title: Bottom-Up Synthesis of Molecular Nanodiamond from Nanographene - Nature
item.description: Bottom-Up Synthesis of Molecular Nanodiamond from Nanographene  Nature

item.title: Large Hadron Collider detects strange particle behavior that could rewrite physics - ScienceDaily
item.description: Large Hadron Collider detects strange particle behavior that could rewrite physics  ScienceDaily
